name: Profile Files
description: Read-only access to everything in the user's profile folder: uploaded documents, saved research, generated images/videos, coder workspace files, and any docs that other users have shared with them.

function.name: list_profile_files
function.description:
List files in the user's profile — uploaded documents, saved research, generated images, generated videos, coder workspace files, AND any docs other users have shared with them (annotated with `[shared by <userId>]`). Returns ids, filenames, sizes, types, timestamps, and sharing status. This is the single tool for ANY 'list my files' / 'show my docs' / 'what's been shared with me' question.

**Default to folder='all' for fuzzy phrasings.** Phrases like 'list my documents', 'show my files', 'what do I have', 'my stuff' are user shorthand for the whole profile, NOT the literal documents subfolder. Only narrow to a specific folder when the user explicitly names a category ("just images", "my videos", "the research markdowns", "code only").

folder.description: Which folder to list. 'all' (default — use this for any non-specific request including 'my documents'). 'documents' = uploaded files (use only when user explicitly says 'just docs' / 'only documents folder'). 'research' = saved research markdowns. 'images' / 'videos' = AI-generated media. 'code' = coder workspace files.

query.description: Optional case-insensitive substring filter applied to filenames and titles.

function.name: read_profile_file
function.description: Read the content of a file from the user's profile. Use the file_id returned by list_profile_files. Returns text for documents/research/code, image data for photos, and a metadata note for videos (which can't be read as text). Caps text output at 50,000 characters.

file_id.description: The file id from list_profile_files (looks like 'documents:doc_abc', 'research:doc_xyz', 'images:filename.png', 'videos:filename.mp4', or 'code:path/to/file.js').
